description_long: This object is a sleek, modern airplane with a streamlined fuselage and sharply swept wings. The wings are mounted low on the body and extend outward with a slight dihedral angle, giving the aircraft a dynamic appearance. Two jet engines are mounted at the rear of the fuselage, positioned on either side of the tail section. The tail features a T-tail configuration, with the horizontal stabilizer mounted atop a single vertical fin. The exterior is primarily white with dark accents on the wings and engine nacelles, and the cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage with a smooth, enclosed canopy. The overall design suggests a business jet or small regional jet, optimized for speed and efficiency.
description: A modern, white airplane with low, swept wings, rear-mounted twin jet engines, and a T-tail. The cockpit is forward and smoothly integrated into the fuselage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, low-mounted swept wing, rear twin jet engines, and prominent T-tail.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, engine placement near the tail, and the T-tail's horizontal stabilizer.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the wing, engines, and tail structure.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the pointed nose, forward cockpit, and the wide, low-mounted wings extending outward.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and slight dihedral angle; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage on either side of the tail.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear; main and nose gear units implied but not visibly extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit integrated smoothly into the nose of the fuselage; likely multi-seat with a streamlined canopy.
